highlights.text: I wrote Wednesday at 4pm: “My general lean is to defer to the trend.
I wrote yesterday at 4pm: “My general lean is always to defer to the trend.
The first principle is that when ES sells it typically goes elevator down and violently flushes every support (we saw this on Monday/Tuesday).
Then, ES short squeezes but this ONLY happens when ES sees a Failed Breakdown (my core setup).
All dips in ES are bought by institutions and institutions buy dips via my core setup: The Failed Breakdown.
While bulls did buy the dip, ES is ultimately just coiling up.
In today’s newsletter I’ll talk this, I’ll go over today’s Failed Breakdowns (these are key to know), and I’ll discuss the actionable plan for tomorrow.

post_title: SPX Is Coiled Tight For A Move. What Way? Jan 26 Plan
